name: Oath of Glory
description: Paladins who take the Oath of Glory believe they and their companions are destined to achieve glory through deeds of heroism. They train diligently and encourage their companions so they're all ready when destiny calls. These Paladins share the following tenets: actions over words, challenges are but tests, and one must hone the body for it is a vessel for the soul.
shortDescription: Strive for the Heights of Heroism

features.name: Oath of Glory Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Glory Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Inspiring Smite
features.description: Immediately after you cast {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity and distribute {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} to creatures of your choice within 30 feet of yourself, which can include you. The total number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equals {@dice 2d8} plus your Paladin level, divided among the chosen creatures however you like.

features.name: Peerless Athlete
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to augment your athleticism. For 1 hour, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) and Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) checks, and the distance of your {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} and {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} increases by 10 feet (this extra distance costs movement as normal).

features.name: Aura of Alacrity
features.description: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet. In addition, whenever an ally enters your Aura of Protection for the first time on a turn or starts their turn there, the ally's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet until the end of their next turn.

features.name: Glorious Defense
features.description: You can turn defense into a sudden strike. When you or another creature you can see within 10 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to add your Charisma modifier to the target's AC against that attack, potentially causing the attack to miss. If the attack misses, you can make one weapon attack against the attacker as part of this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, provided the attacker is within your weapon's range. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Living Legend
features.description: You can empower yourself with the legends—whether true or exaggerated—of your great deeds. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain the benefits below for 10 minutes. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Charismatic.} You are blessed with an otherworldly presence and have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all Charisma checks. {@b Saving Throw Reroll.} If you fail a saving throw, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reroll it. You must use the new roll. {@b Unerring Strike.} Once on each of your turns when you make an attack roll with a weapon and miss, you can cause that attack to hit instead.
